content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


July 9, 1992


Clarence E. Harris
C.E. Harris and Company
3401 West 43rd Street
Los Angeles, California  90008

                                    Re:  Your Request for Confirmation
                                         of Telephone Advice
                                         Our File No. A-92-301

Dear Mr. Harris:

     We have received your letter dated May 19, 1992, seeking
written confirmation of the telephone advice I provided regarding
the contribution limits applicable under the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").1/  Your letter accurately reflects the advice that I
provided.

     You are seeking confirmation of this advice on behalf of
Senator Diane Watson who was a successful candidate in the primary
election for the Board of Supervisors for the County of Los
Angeles and is now up for election in the November general
election for the same seat.  I advised you concerning the decision
in Service Employees International Union, AFL-CIO, et al. v. Fair
Political Practices Commission (9th Cir. 1992) 955 F.2d 1312,
cert. den. ______ U.S. ______ and its effect on the campaign
provisions of the Act.

     As we discussed in our telephone conversation of
May 17, 1992, and a subsequent follow-up conversation, the United
States District Court issued an order in the SEIU case which
invalidated portions of the Act which were added in June of 1988
by Proposition 73.  These invalidated provisions included the
fiscal year contribution limitations of the Act.  As a result of
the court's ruling, the provisions of Proposition 73 providing
fiscal year limits are no longer applicable.

_________________________
1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA  95812 • (916) 322-5660

Advice Letter No. A-92-301
Page 2


     I further advised that the court's ruling did not invalidate
the special election and special runoff election contribution
limits of Proposition 73 because they were based on a per-election
basis.  Therefore, these restrictions remain operative in both
local and statewide special elections. (Section 85305.)

     Moreover, Section 85304 continues to prohibit candidates for
state or local office from transferring campaign funds to any
candidate who is subject to any valid contribution limits,
including those limits established by local jurisdictions and the
Act's special election limits.  However, whether a local ordinance
containing local contribution limits is valid is a question of
local interpretation.

     If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                   Sincerely,

                                   Scott Hallabrin
                                   Acting General Counsel

                                   [signature]
                                   By: Luisa Menchaca
                                       Counsel, Legal Division

SH:LM:lm

sections.question_synthetic: What are the contribution limits applicable under the Political Reform Act following the SEIU v. FPPC court decision that invalidated Proposition 73 fiscal year contribution limitations?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The court's ruling invalidated Proposition 73's fiscal year contribution limits, making them no longer applicable. However, special election and special runoff election contribution limits remain operative on a per-election basis, and candidates cannot transfer campaign funds to candidates subject to valid contribution limits, including local jurisdiction limits.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a confirmation letter documenting previously provided telephone advice. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present; legal question and conclusion are synthesized from the body text. Document addresses impact of SEIU v. FPPC court ruling on campaign contribution provisions.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the contribution limits applicable under the Political Reform Act following the SEIU v. FPPC court decision that invalidated Proposition 73 fiscal year contribution limitations?

CONCLUSION: The court's ruling invalidated Proposition 73's fiscal year contribution limits, making them no longer applicable. However, special election and special runoff election contribution limits remain operative on a per-election basis, and candidates cannot transfer campaign funds to candidates subject to valid contribution limits, including local jurisdiction limits.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice regarding contribution limits under the Political Reform Act following the SEIU court decision that invalidated Proposition 73's fiscal year limitations while preserving special election limits.
